description: Tesla Inc designs, develops, manufactures, and sells electric vehicles, energy generation, and storage systems. It also develops autonomous driving technology.
known_facts: Tesla is the largest pure-play electric vehicle manufacturer in the US
Elon Musk is Tesla's CEO and largest individual shareholder
Tesla operates Gigafactories in Nevada, Texas, Germany, and Shanghai
Autopilot and Full Self-Driving are Tesla's autonomous driving software products
Tesla's energy storage and solar business is a growing secondary segment
Tesla's Model Y was the best-selling car globally in 2023
Supercharger network has over 50,000 chargers worldwide
Tesla began delivering Cybertruck in late 2023
known_risks: NHTSA investigations into Autopilot and Full Self-Driving safety
Elon Musk key-person risk and distraction from other ventures (X, SpaceX, xAI)
Increasing competition from BYD, GM, Ford, and other EV makers
Declining automotive gross margins from aggressive price cuts
Chinese market exposure and BYD competition in home market
Multiple recall notices for over-the-air software safety issues
known_strengths: Vertically integrated manufacturing and battery supply chain
Largest Supercharger network providing charging infrastructure advantage
Software-defined vehicle enabling over-the-air updates and new revenue
First-mover advantage and strong brand in the EV segment
Energy business providing diversification beyond automotive
Dojo supercomputer for training autonomous driving AI
